description_long: This is a narrow, three-story apartment building with a decorative tiled facade in blue and white, typical of Mediterranean or Southern European architecture. The front features two green doors at street level, each topped with a rectangular transom window, and two levels of balconies with ornate white railings and balustrades. The windows are symmetrically arranged in three vertical columns, each with white framing and divided panes. The roof is pitched and covered with reddish-brown tiles, showing some weathering and moss. The sides of the building are also tiled, while the rear is plain and unadorned, with no windows or doors. The overall appearance is elegant and historic, suggesting a residential structure in a dense urban neighborhood.
description: A three-story, narrow apartment building with blue-and-white tiled facade, ornate balconies, and a pitched red-tiled roof. The front is richly detailed, while the sides are tiled and the rear is plain.
description_view_0: Front view showing two green doors, three columns of windows, and two ornate balconies with balustrades.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the blue-and-white tiled wall and the protruding balconies, with the sloped roof visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain, unadorned wall with no windows or doors, and three decorative urns on the roofline.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirroring the other side, with tiled wall and visible balconies, plus the sloped roof.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are symmetrically arranged in three vertical columns across the facade, with each floor featuring three evenly spaced windows aligned horizontally and vertically.
